evidence.span_text: - Medical Reasoning: Patient presents today with symptoms consistent with lateral epicondylitis secondary to overuse.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses mild acute intermittent asthma.
The patient reports right elbow pain.
The clinician assesses high blood sugar.

evidence.span_text: - Medical Reasoning: His blood sugar today is not controlled.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician recommends applying ice to the elbow for at least 20 minutes 3 times per day.

evidence.span_text: - Patient Education and Counseling: The nature of the diagnosis was discussed. He was advised that this is like due to increased tennis activity and was encouraged to rest as much as possible. - Medical Treatment: Patient will discontinue playing tennis for the next 4 to 6 weeks, as well as avoid repetitive stress with the right arm. He will apply ice to the elbow for at least 20 minutes 3 times per day to reduce pain and inflammation. Right forearm brace was provided today to help with discomfort. Prescription for Ultram 50 mg once per day was also provided for pain.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician ordered home blood sugar monitoring twice a day for the next 2 weeks.

evidence.span_text: - Medical Treatment: He will increase his metformin to 1000 mg twice a day. He will begin home blood sugar monitoring twice a day for the next 2 weeks. Hemoglobin A1c, CBC, and a Chem-12 test were ordered today.
